target: F64 comparison eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge (same-shape + both scalar-broadcast orders) -> Bool

lever.description: Add F64 fast paths to eval_comparison: eval_same_shape_f64_compare for same-shape F64 tensors and eval_f64_scalar_compare for the Scalar⊗Tensor / Tensor⊗Scalar broadcast branches (scalar_on_left preserves order). Both apply the already-provided float_cmp closure directly to f64::from_bits values, producing Literal::Bool, skipping the complex-operand check and per-element literal_to_i128 integral probe in compare_literals. Non-F64 operands return None and fall through to the generic path.

baseline.note: fast-path guards temporarily removed; generic compare_literals path

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/(eq_1k_f64|lt_scalar_1k_f64)_vec'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output elements produced in tensor index order; scalar_on_left preserves Scalar⊗Tensor vs Tensor⊗Scalar operand order for the asymmetric ordered comparisons.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Each output bool is float_cmp(from_bits(a), from_bits(b)) with the same closure the generic path uses for F64; comparisons are exact and deterministic.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: None introduced. NaN ordering (false for eq/lt/le/gt/ge, true for ne) and -0.0 == 0.0 preserved.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only F64 same-shape and F64 scalar/F64 tensor comparisons take the fast path; other dtypes/malformed literals/multi-dim broadcasts return None and use the generic path.
isomorphism_proof.unit_test: fj-lax comparison::tests::f64_compare_fast_paths_bit_identical_to_scalar (all 6 ops, both orders, NaN/+-inf/-0.0); 994/994 fj-lax tests pass.
